title: Palm Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## Palm Sunday, March 28, 2021 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Palm Sunday 2021 fell on March 28, marking the beginning of Holy Week and the final Sunday of Lent in the church calendar. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the primary readings would have been Matthew 21:1-11 (Jesus's triumphal entry into Jerusalem) and Isaiah 50:4-7, with Psalm 118:1-2 and 19-29. This Sunday traditionally commemorates Jesus's entry into Jerusalem with palm branches while also foreshadowing his Passion, creating the liturgical tension between triumph and suffering that would characterize the week ahead.

---

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the midst of significant pandemic-related adaptations to worship and community life, with many dioceses still operating under COVID-19 protocols established in 2020. The Diocese of Colorado, which oversees Saint John's Cathedral, was navigating reopening procedures and hybrid in-person/online worship models that had become standard practice by spring 2021. No major denominational crises or General Convention actions were unfolding at this precise moment, though the broader church continued processing racial justice commitments made following the summer 2020 protests.

---

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early spring weather with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit, typical for late March in Colorado. The city remained under relatively moderate COVID-19 restrictions compared to winter lockdowns, with vaccination rates beginning to increase as the J&J and Moderna vaccines became more widely available in Colorado. Local attention was focused on the upcoming Colorado legislative session and ongoing debates about pandemic recovery, education reopening, and homelessness in downtown Denver.

---

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The United States was approximately one month into President Biden's administration, with ongoing legislative focus on the $1.9 trillion American Rescue Plan (passed March 10). The nation was accelerating COVID-19 vaccination efforts, with eligibility expanding to include more age groups and essential workers. The trial of Derek Chauvin for George Floyd's murder was underway in Minneapolis, with closing arguments and jury deliberations scheduled for the week following this sermon, making issues of racial justice and policing prominently in the national consciousness.

---

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

India was experiencing a devastating surge in COVID-19 cases, with daily infection counts rising dramatically through March and becoming a global humanitarian crisis by April. The Suez Canal remained a major news story following the March 23 grounding of the Ever Given container ship, which had only recently been refloated and was affecting global supply chains. The Israel-Palestine conflict was entering a period of escalating tensions, with rocket fire and Israeli military responses intensifying in the weeks following this date.

---

CULTURAL MOMENT

American society in late March 2021 occupied a peculiar threshold between pandemic exhaustion and tentative hope—vaccination rates were climbing, restrictions were loosening, but COVID-19 remained a daily reality and a source of anxiety. The cultural moment was characterized by wariness, grief fatigue, and a kind of cautious anticipation of "return to normal," though that phrase itself remained contested. For a Denver congregation, particularly one in an urban Episcopal cathedral setting, the convergence of Palm Sunday's themes of political upheaval and suffering would have resonated with ongoing national reckoning with racial injustice and the trial's imminent conclusion.
